structure.description: Canvas with a top-level scope toggle: Global (force-directed over entire graph) ⇄ Local (k-hop neighborhood around current entity). Local mode reads `centerNoteId` from the current detail context; Global ignores it. Hop-count slider visible only in local mode.

rationale.hypothesis: Global graphs are visually impressive but cognitively useless past ~200 nodes — the layout becomes a hairball. Local graphs are useful for orientation but lose the macro structure. Offering both as one-click peers, with the same filter/color controls applied, lets the user zoom in/out of structure without rebuilding mental context.

evidence.description: Graph view has global mode + 'Local Graph' core plugin with hops + linked/unlinked filters; both share filter chips
Global graph + page-graph (local) with depth control

counterexample.description: No graph view at all — pattern doesn't apply to linear-process products
Single canvas without scope concept — graph is the document, not a derived view

shouldMatch.reason: If notion adds graph projection over Page.parentPageId + Block.targetPageId, dual-scope fits

shouldNotMatch.reason: Workflow graph is the artifact, not a derived view; scope toggle has no meaning
No relational graph between assets at user-level
